2603.28351.title: Not All Subjectivity Is the Same! Defining Desiderata for the Evaluation of Subjectivity in NLP
2603.28351.paper_summary: Subjective judgments are part of several NLP datasets and recent work is increasingly prioritizing models whose outputs reflect this diversity of perspectives. Such responses allow us to shed light on minority voices, which are frequently marginalized or obscured by dominant perspectives. It remains a question whether our evaluation practices align with these models' objectives. This position paper proposes seven evaluation desiderata for subjectivity-sensitive models, rooted in how subjectivity is represented in NLP data and models. The desiderata are constructed in a top-down approach, keeping in mind the user-centric impact of such models. We scan the experimental setup of 60 papers and show that various aspects of subjectivity are still understudied: the distinction between ambiguous and polyphonic input, whether subjectivity is effectively expressed to the user, and a lack of interplay between different desiderata, amongst other gaps.
2603.28351.paper_summary_zh: 主觀判斷是幾個自然語言處理（NLP）數據集的一部分，最近的研究越來越重視那些其輸出反映這種多樣性觀點的模型。這樣的回應使我們能夠揭示少數聲音，這些聲音經常被主流觀點邊緣化或掩蓋。是否我們的評估實踐與這些模型的目標一致仍然是一個問題。這篇立場論文提出了七個針對敏感主觀性的模型的評估期望，這些期望根植於主觀性在NLP數據和模型中的表現方式。這些期望是以自上而下的方法構建的，考慮到這些模型對用戶的影響。我們掃描了60篇論文的實驗設置，顯示出主觀性的各個方面仍然研究不足：模糊和多聲音輸入之間的區別、主觀性是否有效地表達給用戶，以及不同期望之間缺乏相互作用等其他差距。
2603.28351.author: Urja Khurana et.al.
2603.28351.authors: Urja Khurana, Michiel van der Meer, Enrico Liscio, Antske Fokkens, Pradeep K. Murukannaiah
